name: Pinnacle Bank
description_short: Pinnacle Financial Partners is a $119.1B regional bank offering comprehensive banking, investment, trust, mortgage and insurance products across the Southeast and Mid-Atlantic.
description_long:
Pinnacle Financial Partners, Inc. is a major regional bank headquartered in Tennessee with over 160 years of combined banking service following its 2026 merger with Synovus Financial Corp. The combined entity manages $119.1 billion in assets and operates as the largest bank headquartered in Tennessee and the largest bank holding company headquartered in Georgia, with branch locations across Tennessee, Georgia, Florida, North Carolina, South Carolina, Alabama, Kentucky, Virginia and Maryland.

Pinnacle provides a full range of banking services including checking and savings accounts, online banking, credit cards (including the Grizzlies Credit Card), ACH services, remote deposit, merchant services, dealer floor plan financing, lockbox services, positive pay, and integrated receivables solutions. They offer investment management, trust services, mortgage products, and insurance solutions for both commercial and consumer clients. The bank also provides specialized business banking tools including employer portals, merchant online reporting, PCI compliance support, and loan payment systems.

Pinnacle distinguishes itself through significant industry recognition, earning 50 Coalition Greenwich 2026 Best Bank Awards for service in 2025—more than any other bank in the country. The awards span categories most correlated with client loyalty including Overall Satisfaction, Likelihood to Recommend, Ease of Doing Business, Bank You Can Trust, and Values Long-term Relationships. The company was also recognized as No. 9 on FORTUNE magazine's 2025 list of 100 Best Companies to Work For, indicating strong organizational culture and employee satisfaction.

As a major regional bank, Pinnacle offers traditional banking stability, FDIC insurance protection, and comprehensive financial services under one roof. However, as a large institution, it may lack the personalized attention of community banks, and consumers seeking alternative banking may find limited neobank features. The company's primary strength lies in serving small business and middle market clients across the Southeast and Mid-Atlantic regions rather than serving underserved populations or offering innovative fintech solutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal and business checking and savings accounts
Credit cards including Grizzlies branded card
Merchant services with online reporting and PCI compliance
Mortgage lending for home purchase and refinance
pros: Earned 50 Coalition Greenwich 2026 Best Bank Awards—more than any other bank in the country, validating service quality
FDIC insured and regulated by the Tennessee Department of Financial Institutions, providing consumer protection
Ranked No. 9 on FORTUNE's 100 Best Companies to Work For, indicating strong workplace culture and stability
Comprehensive service offerings including banking, investments, trust, mortgages, and insurance all in one institution
Extensive geographic footprint with 9-state presence and multiple ATM locations for convenient access
Specialized business banking tools including merchant services, ACH, lockbox, and integrated receivables
Strong market position with No. 1 deposit market share in Nashville MSA and No. 4 in Atlanta MSA
cons: Large regional institution may lack personalized relationship banking experience of smaller community banks
No mention of digital-first or neobank features, suggesting traditional banking platform focus
Limited information about consumer loan products, credit cards, or retail banking offerings on primary website
Regional concentration in Southeast and Mid-Atlantic limits accessibility for consumers in other parts of the country
No evidence of fintech innovation, mobile-first design, or alternative financial products for tech-savvy consumers
best_for: Small business and middle market clients seeking comprehensive banking and advisory relationships in the Southeast/Mid-Atlantic
Commercial clients needing integrated merchant services, lockbox, dealer floor plan, and receivables management
Consumers in Tennessee, Georgia, Florida, or neighboring states wanting full-service traditional banking with strong local presence
Business owners prioritizing relationship banking and long-term financial partnerships with established institutions
diagnosis: Pinnacle Financial Partners is best for small business owners, middle market companies, and consumers in the Southeast and Mid-Atlantic seeking comprehensive, relationship-based banking from an established regional institution. The main caveat is that as a traditional large bank, it prioritizes commercial and middle-market clients over consumer retail banking, and offers limited indication of fintech innovation or mobile-first banking experiences for digitally-native consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
